message: packet access reaches the use before the verifier has enough range for the extension-derived L4 pointer: invalid access to packet, off=64 size=2, R3(id=1,off=64,r=0); R3 offset is outside of the packet
required_proof: prove the actual IPv6 extension-derived L4 packet pointer has room for struct udphdr before reading udp->dest

related_spans.source_text: checked_udp = fixed_l4; if ((void *)(checked_udp + 1) > data_end) return XDP_PASS;
related_spans.label: bounds are established only for the fixed no-extension L4 pointer
inline extension parsing can move l4 by a packet-derived extension length

evidence.detail: invalid access to packet, off=64 size=2, R3(id=1,off=64,r=0); R3 offset is outside of the packet
R3 is a packet pointer with variable offset from the hop-by-hop extension length, while the checked packet pointer is fixed at the no-extension L4 offset

help: After extension parsing, check `(void *)(l4 + sizeof(struct udphdr)) <= data_end` before assigning `udp = l4` or reading `udp->dest`.
Do not remove hop-by-hop extension parsing; packets with IPv6 extension headers must still apply the UDP port policy at the final L4 offset.
